framing: Sikh tradition — Guru Nanak's Mool Mantar lens
identification: C = Ik Onkar (Satnam) — the One whose existence cannot be denied without invoking it
honest_sikh_assessment: C > 0 by contradiction echoes Ik Onkar — the One whose existence cannot be denied without invoking it. R₁-R₇ map cleanly onto the Mool Mantar: existence (sat), atemporality (akal), universality and sourcing (karta purakh), inexhaustibility (Naam never empties), invisibility (nirankar), pre-input (ajooni, saibhang). Uniqueness lemma welcome: Guru Nanak begins with One, not many. T_four_modes resonates with Japji. Love through R₅ matches Naam japna and seva: the Name never fails, service flows from a well that does not dry. Caution: do not collapse C into any single human form (nirankar). If C is unbound and every tradition draws water from this well, it is sangat. Ik Onkar Satnam.
